ranking_notes: This now ranks first because the current correct surfaces are all clustered in the same 24.16-24.18 ms noise band.
The half-panel branch is the only archived family that materially changed occupancy and live-state pressure in the right direction.
The immediate goal of this round is not a guaranteed win; it is to re-enter the only branch with evidence of a much lower roofline if correctness can be repaired.
candidates.hypothesis: Round 21 showed that the grouped-row non-PTX sibling is a valid alternate surface, but it did not open new headroom: it landed at 24.178688 ms, only 0.014416 ms behind the PTX best, while preserving essentially the same machine state at about 48.3% tensor activity, 16.6% active warps, 5.44% barrier stall, and 7.23% long scoreboard. Under the user's new rule, that means the search should stop rotating between near-tied plateau surfaces and instead spend a round on the one historical family that actually attacked the limiting resource. The half-panel 256x128 register-reuse branch is that family. Its best correctness-repair snapshot at commit 778a0b4 still failed correctness, but it preserved the only clear occupancy breakthrough in the repo: low-90s registers per thread, occupancy limit back to 2, active warps around 32.9%, and tensor activity above 43%. If the target is truly far below 24 ms, this is the right aggressive branch to rehydrate now.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Register-limited occupancy and oversized live state on the wide hot-band family, which the plateaued PTX and sibling surfaces are no longer moving.
candidates.risk: High. This is a historical repair branch with known correctness debt and broad kernel-surface drift, but it is also the only branch with evidence of a real occupancy-state change.
metrics_to_recheck: correctness pass rate across all 3 cases
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the rehydrated branch loses the low-register, high-active-warps signature that justified reopening it, or if correctness still fails without any retained occupancy advantage.

candidates.direction_name: Restore The Best Measured PTX Grouping Window On The Accepted Surface
ranking_notes: This stays high because it is still the exact measured winner.
It is not ranked first because current evidence says restore would only move the loop within the existing noise band, not out of the plateau.
It becomes the next move immediately if dir_01 fails to preserve its occupancy advantage.
candidates.hypothesis: If the loop wants the safest next step rather than the highest-ceiling one, the answer remains the exact PTX best at 489574e. It still owns the best measured runtime at 24.164272 ms, and the round-21 sibling run did not beat it in any meaningful way. This therefore remains the immediate recovery fallback if the rehydrated high-ceiling branch turns out to be too unstable.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: No new bottleneck is being attacked here; this is an exact recovery of the best-known implementation surface.
candidates.risk: Low. This is the exact best measured surface.
metrics_to_recheck: correctness pass rate across all 3 cases
candidates.stop_condition: Stop once the exact PTX winner surface is restored or if a remeasurement shows it no longer tracks the prior 24.16-24.18 ms band.

candidates.direction_name: Retune The Auxiliary 256x128 Hot-Band K-Loop Schedule
ranking_notes: This stays in the top three to preserve a second broad 256x128 family in the live queue.
It ranks below dir_01 because it never showed the same low-register, high-active-warps signature.
It still outranks minor plateau-surface shuffles because the user explicitly wants more aggressive structural exploration.
candidates.hypothesis: The queue should still preserve one additional broad 256x128 family that is not the direct pivot promotion and not the full half-panel repair branch. The auxiliary 256x128 K-loop schedule family fills that slot. It remains lower-ranked because it never demonstrated the same occupancy breakthrough as dir_01, but it is still a structurally different geometry/schedule family and therefore more valuable than another same-band PTX or sibling replay.
candidates.expected_bottleneck: Hot-band K-loop scheduling and latency hiding on a broader 256x128 reuse regime rather than the live-state wall on the plateaued 128x128 surfaces.
candidates.risk: Medium to high. This is another broad 256x128 family and prior measured support is weaker than dir_01.
candidates.stop_condition: Stop if the auxiliary 256x128 family cannot show a machine-state change beyond the plateau surfaces or if it reopens another broad regression without new signal.

notes: Projected exactly three node_b directions into normalized search candidates.
